days.title: Talking about yesterday: -л / -ла

grammar.title: Past tense of -ть verbs: -л and -ла
grammar.explanation:
To make the past tense, drop **-ть** from the infinitive and add a past-tense ending that agrees with the **gender of the subject**.

- A **male** speaker/subject takes **-л**: я/ты/он работа**л**.
- A **female** speaker/subject takes **-ла**: я/ты/она работа**ла**.

The ending matches gender, not person, so **я работал** (man) vs **я работала** (woman). Example: чита**ть** → чита**л** / чита**ла**.

reading.title: Что ты делал?
reading.text: — Что ты делал вчера? — Я читал книгу и слушал музыку.
reading.translation: "What did you do yesterday?" "I read a book and listened to music."

days.speakingPrompt: Say two things you did yesterday using -л or -ла, for example: «Вчера я читал книгу» and «Я слушал музыку». Use the correct ending for your own gender.

days.title: Neuter and plural: -ло and -ли

grammar.title: Past tense: -ло (neuter) and -ли (plural)
grammar.explanation:
Two more past-tense endings complete the set:

- **Neuter** subjects (оно, words like радио) take **-ло**: радио игра**ло**.
- **Plural** subjects (они, мы, вы, plural nouns) take **-ли** — gender does not matter here: они работа**ли**, дети игра**ли**.

So every verb has four past forms: работа**л** / работа**ла** / работа**ло** / работа**ли**. Choose by the subject.

reading.title: Вчера дома
reading.text: Вчера дети играли дома. Родители работали, а потом все гуляли вместе.
reading.translation: Yesterday the children played at home. The parents worked, and then everyone walked together.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe what your family or friends did yesterday using -ли, for example: «Вчера мы гуляли вместе» or «Дети играли дома». Try to use both гулять and играть.

days.title: When it happened: time markers

vocab.english: in the afternoon, during the day

grammar.title: Time-of-day markers with the past tense
grammar.explanation:
Russian uses single adverbs to say **when** something happened: **утром** (in the morning), **днём** (in the afternoon), **вечером** (in the evening), **ночью** (at night). They usually come at the **start** of the sentence or right before the verb.

These pair naturally with past-tense verbs you already know. **потом** (then) links one action to the next.

examples.russian: Днём мы гуляли, а вечером отдыхали.
examples.english: In the afternoon we walked, and in the evening we rested.

examples.russian: Сначала я читал, потом слушал музыку.
examples.english: First I read, then I listened to music.

reading.title: Мой день
reading.text: Утром я работал. Днём мы гуляли, а вечером отдыхали дома.
reading.translation: In the morning I worked. In the afternoon we walked, and in the evening we rested at home.

days.speakingPrompt: Tell the story of your day yesterday using утром, днём and вечером, for example: «Утром я работал, днём гулял, а вечером отдыхал».

days.title: How things were: был / была / было / были

grammar.title: The past tense of "to be": был / была / было / были
grammar.explanation:
Russian has **no** word for "is" in the present, but in the past it uses **был** for "was/were". It behaves like any past verb and agrees with the subject's gender and number:

- masculine → **был** (дом был большой)
- feminine → **была** (погода была хорошая)
- neuter → **было** (утро было тёплое)
- plural → **были** (дети были дома)

The adjective also agrees with the noun: хоро**ший** дом, хоро**шая** погода, тёпл**ое** утро.

reading.title: Хороший день
reading.text: Вчера погода была хорошая. Утро было тёплое, а вечер холодный.
reading.translation: Yesterday the weather was good. The morning was warm, but the evening was cold.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe yesterday's weather and your day using был/была/было, for example: «Вчера погода была хорошая. День был хороший».

days.title: Places in the past: был in / был at

vocab.exampleEn: In the morning the children were at school.
In the afternoon I was at work.
In the evening we were in the park.
In the morning mum was in the shop.

grammar.title: Saying where you were: был + в/на + place
grammar.explanation:
To say where someone **was**, use был/была/было/были with **в** (in) or **на** (at) and the location. After в/на the noun takes the **prepositional ending -е** (you saw this in present tense too): школа → в школ**е**, работа → на работ**е**, парк → в парк**е**, магазин → в магазин**е**.

Use **раньше** (before/earlier) to contrast the past with now.

examples.english: In the morning the children were at school.
In the afternoon I was at work.
In the evening we were in the park.

reading.title: Где все были?
reading.text: Утром дети были в школе, а мама в магазине. Днём все были на работе.
reading.translation: In the morning the children were at school, and mum was in the shop. In the afternoon everyone was at work.

days.speakingPrompt: Say where you were yesterday morning, afternoon and evening, for example: «Утром я был на работе, а вечером в парке». Use в or на with the -е ending.

days.title: Yes I did, no I didn't: negative past

vocab.example: Я никогда не был в Москве.
vocab.exampleEn: I have never been to Moscow.
We walked for a long time.
In the evening we watched television.

grammar.title: Negative past tense with не
grammar.explanation:
To say something **didn't** happen, put **не** directly before the past-tense verb: я **не** работал = "I didn't work". The verb still agrees with gender/number as usual.

With был, не goes before it too: я **не** был дома. The word **никогда** (never) is used together with **не**: я **никогда не был** в Москве — Russian uses a double negative, and that is correct here.

examples.russian: Я никогда не был в Москве.
examples.english: I have never been to Moscow.

reading.title: Тихий вечер
reading.text: Вчера вечером я не работал. Я немного смотрел телевизор, а потом долго отдыхал.
reading.translation: Yesterday evening I didn't work. I watched television a little, and then rested for a long time.

days.speakingPrompt: Say one thing you did and one thing you did NOT do yesterday, for example: «Вечером я смотрел телевизор, но не работал». Try to use не and a time marker.

days.title: Review: The past week

grammar.title: Recap: the past tense and был
grammar.explanation:
This week you learned how to talk about **the past**:

- Past tense = drop **-ть** and add **-л** (he), **-ла** (she), **-ло** (it), **-ли** (they/plural): работал, работала, работало, работали.
- **был / была / было / были** = "was/were", agreeing with gender and number.
- Place: был **в/на** + noun with **-е**: в школе, на работе, в парке.
- Negative: **не** before the verb; **никогда не** = never.
- Time markers: **вчера, раньше, утром, днём, вечером, ночью, потом**.

examples.russian: Вчера утром я работал, а вечером отдыхал.
examples.english: Yesterday morning I worked, and in the evening I rested.

examples.russian: Погода была хорошая, и мы долго гуляли.
examples.english: The weather was good, and we walked for a long time.

examples.russian: Раньше дети были в школе, а потом дома.
examples.english: Earlier the children were at school, and then at home.

reading.title: Как прошёл день?
reading.text: — Что ты делал вчера? — Утром я был на работе, а днём мы гуляли в парке. Погода была хорошая. — А вечером? — Вечером я не работал, я смотрел телевизор и отдыхал.
reading.translation: "What did you do yesterday?" "In the morning I was at work, and in the afternoon we walked in the park. The weather was good." "And in the evening?" "In the evening I didn't work, I watched television and rested."

days.speakingPrompt: Tell the full story of your day yesterday from morning to evening. Use at least three time markers (утром, днём, вечером), past-tense verbs, был/была for where you were and what the weather was like, and one negative sentence with не.
